Why Serenity

Why Serenity

Four principles shape every session — built around you, refined as you go.

01   Client first

You set the pace.

Every session starts with your body, your goals, your history, your day. We listen before we prescribe and adapt in real time.

02   Method

APPI-trained & personalised.

Evidence-based programmes built around your body. Recovery, mobility, or strength. Your plan evolves as you progress.

03   Equipment

Premium Align Pilates.

Smooth, precise, progressive. Align Pilates reformers and apparatus, built for clean movement and real strength.

04   Progress

Progress, tracked.

Range, control, strength. Measured monthly so every session builds meaningfully on the last.

What is Reformer Pilates?

A sliding carriage. Adjustable springs. Movement that meets you exactly where you are. Reformer Pilates is a mindful movement method using specialised equipment to help your body move better and feel better. The reformer is a sliding carriage machine with adjustable spring resistance, ropes, and straps that allow for smooth, controlled movement through a wide range of motion. Where a gym pushes load and a yoga class focuses on held postures, reformer Pilates lives in the middle: low impact, high control, and tailored to your body. It suits almost anyone, including those returning from injury, busy professionals, fitness enthusiasts, and seniors staying active.

Get stronger Return from injury Busy professionals Move smarter Stay active at any age
Our equipment

Premium apparatus. Cleaner movement.

01

Align Pilates Reformer

Sliding carriage, adjustable spring resistance — the beating heart of the studio. Every session begins and ends here.

Spring Resistance Carriage Work Full Body
02

Arc Barrel

Opens the spine and chest, deepens core work through guided extension. Perfect for backbends and spinal articulation.

Spinal Extension Core Depth Flexibility
03

Spine Corrector

Targeted postural realignment and spinal mobility for rehab work. Ideal for clients recovering from injury or managing chronic tension.

Postural Work Rehab Mobility

The instructors

Trained. Certified. Genuinely invested.

Portrait — to be added
Sneha AjithFounder · Lead Instructor
Founder · Lead Instructor

Sneha Ajith

APPI-trained, practising since 2017. A deeply personal approach to movement — built from a lifetime in dance, Kalari, and martial arts.

Sneha's background includes classical dance, Kalaripayattu, boxing, and Muay Thai. Having overcome her own movement-related injuries through Pilates, she teaches with a focus on mindful movement, strength with control, and a deeper connection with the body.

She has also appeared on screen as an actor — bringing the same focus and presence to her work in film.

APPI CertifiedSince 2017Dance · Kalari · Muay Thai
Portrait — to be added
SoorajInstructor
Instructor

Sooraj

APPI-trained. Mr. Kerala 2019. Specialises in the intersection of performance and recovery.

A multidisciplinary fitness expert, Sooraj integrates evidence-based Pilates with a deep background in bodybuilding, Karate, and Judo.

His holistic approach prioritises long-term health and optimal body mechanics — focusing on rehabilitation, corrective exercise, and functional conditioning to take clients from injury recovery to peak athletic performance.

APPI CertifiedMr. Kerala 2019Bodybuilding · Karate · Judo

Good to know

Questions, answered.

Still unsure? Message us on WhatsApp — we're happy to help before your first visit.

Reformer uses spring-loaded resistance for strength, mobility, stability, posture correction, and injury prevention. More progressive than mat, more dynamic than yoga's held postures.
Yes — changing rooms and a shower available at the studio.
Cancel at least 24 hours before your class.
Almost certainly. Springs support before they challenge, making reformer one of the most beginner-friendly forms of strength work. Start with the intro private session (₹900) one-on-one.

Ready to move?

Quickest way to book — WhatsApp us. Or drop an email, we'll get back same day.
